title: BridgeClient spawns a fresh Python interpreter for every gated Pi tool call
evidence: BridgeClient.call() (bridge.ts:269-338) does `child = spawn(paths.python, [...pythonPrefixArgs, paths.script], {...})` inside the Promise body on every invocation — there is no persistent/pooled Python process and no reuse across calls. tool-guard.ts:131-147 calls `bridge.call({event:'tool_call', ...})` from inside every wrapped bash/write/edit/read tool's `execute()`, i.e. once per PreToolUse-equivalent gate in a Pi session. Each spawned process pays full CPython interpreter startup, then pi-bridge.py imports _host and _prunepolicy at module load (pi-bridge.py:14-17) and, per request, dynamically loads and executes the routed hook module via importlib (`_load_entry`, pi-bridge.py:143-154), which itself imports hostapi, _gitlib, _hooklib, etc. (see pre-bash.py:22-35) — a fresh cold import graph every call.
impact: Every single Bash/Write/Edit/Read tool call in a Pi session incurs a full Python process spawn + interpreter cold start + hook module import graph before the tool itself executes, on top of the Node spawn/IPC overhead already paid. On Windows in particular, process creation is expensive (tens of ms), so this is a fixed per-call latency tax applied uniformly across an entire session's tool calls — exactly the kind of hot-path cost the repo's own #194 SessionStart fast path was built to avoid for the Claude Code side, but here it recurs on EVERY tool call rather than once per session.
recommendation: Introduce a long-lived Python worker process per Pi session (spawned once at session_start, fed one JSON request per line over its stdin/stdout, matching the existing bounded-protocol shape pi-bridge.py already uses) instead of spawning+tearing down a process per tool call; fall back to the current spawn-per-call path if the worker dies, preserving the existing fail-closed/timeout semantics.
acceptance_criteria: A single Pi session issuing N gated tool calls spawns O(1) Python processes rather than O(N)

Bridge round-trip latency for a warm session is measurably lower than the current per-call cold-start baseline (captured by an updated pi_benchmark.py that exercises the real BridgeClient, not a stubbed bridge)
Existing fail-closed/timeout/kill-tree behavior on bridge failure is preserved for the worker model
